1._: Cadell comes up the lowland drove-road to where the three great ways meet, spear cased and shield slung, and halts at the flat-topped gather-stone to take the measure of the slate roofs and the smoke of the settlement below. Two others have come to the same crossroads off other roads — he clocks them the way he clocks anyone: how they would stand in a fight. Fresh marks are scratched into the stone's face in no hand he knows; he notes them and lets them be. Coin and trouble are down in the hall, not up here at a stone.

content._: cadell's account at Thousand Valleys, beat by beat.
